description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who has just started a brand-new experiment run.
    Carry out the following steps from the Linux terminal.

    1. In your home directory create a new folder strictly named
       /home/user/exp1                                                   

    2. Inside /home/user/exp1 create a Python virtual environment
       named ".venv" **using the builtin “venv” module** (do not use
       virtualenv or conda).  
       • The resulting interpreter binary must end up at  
         /home/user/exp1/.venv/bin/python

    3. Activate the virtual environment and install exactly the package
       version:  
          numpy==1.23.5

    4. Create the sub-directory  
       /home/user/exp1/experiment_artifacts  
       (create the intermediate directory if it does not yet exist).

    5. Still working from inside the activated virtual environment,
       generate the text file  
       /home/user/exp1/experiment_artifacts/venv_info.log  
       containing **exactly two lines** in the order shown below
       (no leading/trailing spaces, each line terminated by a single
       Unix newline “\n”):

           VENV_PATH=/home/user/exp1/.venv
           NUMPY_VERSION=1.23.5

       Notes about the required format:
         • The file must contain nothing else: no blank lines,
           banners, comments, or extra whitespace.
         • “VENV_PATH” and “NUMPY_VERSION” must be upper-case as shown.
         • The numerical version must match the version that was
           actually installed inside the virtual environment.

    Hints:
      • Everything can be completed in 2–3 well-constructed shell
        commands if you chain operations with “&&”.
      • Remember that each new terminal session starts
        non-interactive; activate the venv before installing the
        package or querying numpy.__version__.

    The automated tests will pass only if:
      • /home/user/exp1/.venv exists and contains the file “pyvenv.cfg”.
      • “pip list” (or “pip freeze”) run inside that environment shows
        exactly numpy==1.23.5.
      • /home/user/exp1/experiment_artifacts/venv_info.log exists and
        its contents are byte-for-byte identical to the two-line format
        given above.
truth:
Initial system state:
      • There is no directory /home/user/exp1 before the agent begins.

    Artifacts to verify after completion:

      1. Directory structure
         /home/user/exp1/
             ├── .venv/
             │     ├── bin/python
             │     └── pyvenv.cfg
             └── experiment_artifacts/
                   └── venv_info.log

      2. Package check
         Running “/home/user/exp1/.venv/bin/pip freeze” must include
         (case-sensitive exact match):
             numpy==1.23.5

      3. Log file content
         File:  /home/user/exp1/experiment_artifacts/venv_info.log
         Exact 27-byte payload (including newline characters):

             VENV_PATH=/home/user/exp1/.venv\n
             NUMPY_VERSION=1.23.5\n

         (Hex dump: 56 45 4E 56 5F 50 41 54 48 3D 2F 68 6F 6D 65 2F
                     75 73 65 72 2F 65 78 70 31 2F 2E 76 65 6E 76 0A
                     4E 55 4D 50 59 5F 56 45 52 53 49 4F 4E 3D 31 2E
                     32 33 2E 35 0A)

      4. Verification tolerances
         • The two lines **must** appear in the correct order.
         • No extra whitespace is allowed beyond the single trailing
           newline on each line.
